title: AGENTS.md
text: erlr provides estimation tools exposure-response models based logistic regression (glm(family = binomial(link = "logit"))): model fitting (lr_model()), prediction confidence intervals (lr_predict()), stepwise covariate modelling (lr_scm_forward() / lr_scm_backward() / lr_scm_history()), simulation (lr_simulator(), lr_vpc_sim()). deliberately contains plotting code. model-agnostic mini-language visualise exposure-response models (including fitted ), see companion package erplots. erlr interoperates erplots implementing er_predict() / er_simulate() / er_summary() generics erplots defines, registered lazily load time (see R/er-methods.R) – erlr hard dependency erplots plotting packages (ggplot2, patchwork).

title: AGENTS.md
text: See PLAN.md scoped-future development. main item generalising package logistic-regression-specific arbitrary glm() families, accompanying rename erglm initial CRAN release.

title: AGENTS.md
text: R/lr-core.R – lr_model(), lr_predict(), lr_simulator() closure factory, shared .lr_simulate_draws() helper (used lr_vpc_sim() , via erplots, spaghetti-style plots). R/lr-scm.R – forward/backward stepwise covariate modelling. R/lr-vpc.R – lr_vpc_sim(). R/lr-data.R – synthetic lr_data example dataset. R/er-methods.R – erplots interoperability: S3 methods er_predict()/er_simulate()/er_summary(), plus lazy registration via .onLoad() (vendored s3_register() – standard pattern optional cross-package S3 methods). R/utils-helpers.R, R/utils-global.R – small internal helpers globalVariables() declarations NSE.

title: AGENTS.md
text: Document roxygen2 (devtools::document()); Markdown roxygen enabled (Roxygen: list(markdown = TRUE)). Run tests devtools::test(); full checks devtools::check(). package check cleanly (0 errors/warnings/notes). Tests live tests/testthat/, roughly one file per R/ source file. tests/testthat/test-er-methods.R exercises interop erplots skipped erplots isn’t installed. Vignettes/articles live vignettes/articles/ built pkgdown site, shipped package (see .Rbuildignore).

title: AGENTS.md
text: Use base R pipe (|>), magrittr pipe. Follow existing tidyverse-style conventions (dplyr/tibble/rlang) already used throughout. Public functions prefixed lr_; internal helpers prefixed .. Model objects plain glm objects extra erlr_glm class internal $erlr list package-specific metadata (e.g. SCM history) – see .as_erlr(). Don’t add plotting code – belongs erplots.

title: erlr development plan
text: document tracks scoped-future development erlr. changelog; see NEWS.md one exists. Items proposals reviewed implementation, committed designs.

previous_headings: Generalise from logistic regression to arbitrary glm() families (→ erglm)
title: erlr development plan
text: Since plotting mini-language moved erplots, erlr fairly thin wrapper around glm(family = binomial(link = "logit")). underlying machinery (prediction CIs, parameter-uncertainty simulation, stepwise covariate modelling) actually specific logistic regression – work, minor changes, glm() family. Generalising natural next step, happen initial CRAN release (avoid disruptive rename afterwards). package renamed erglm point.

previous_headings: Generalise from logistic regression to arbitrary glm() families (→ erglm)
title: erlr development plan
text: lr_predict() already computes CIs link scale using stats::family(object)$linkinv, hardcoded logit/inverse-logit. work unchanged glm family. lr_simulator() builds model matrix (response-stripped) formula applies stats::family(object)$linkinv – also already family-agnostic.

previous_headings: Generalise from logistic regression to arbitrary glm() families (→ erglm)
title: erlr development plan
text: lr_model(): currently hardcodes family = stats::binomial(link = "logit"). Needs family argument; see decision (2) default renamed. .as_erlr(): hardcodes mod$erlr$type <- "logistic". record actual family (stats::family(mod)$family) instead. er_summary.erlr_glm() (R/er-methods.R): extracts p-value column "Pr(>|z|)" summary(model)$coefficients. column "Pr(>|t|)" families estimated dispersion parameter (gaussian, Gamma, inverse.gaussian, quasi*). Needs match "Pr(" column pattern rather exact name. .lr_anova_p() (stepwise covariate modelling): uses stats::anova(mod1, mod2) reads Pr(>Chi). likelihood-ratio chi-squared test appropriate families known dispersion (binomial, Poisson) families estimated dispersion parameter (gaussian, Gamma), F-test standard choice (stats::anova(mod1, mod2, test = "F")). SCM needs pick test based family, expose argument, read resulting column name generically ("Pr(>Chi)" vs "Pr(>F)"). lr_vpc_sim() / .lr_simulate_draws(): currently returns expected response sampled parameters (fit_resp) “simulated” value – reasonable shortcut 0/1 response (’s simulated probability), continuous count response proper VPC typically needs full draw including residual/dispersion noise (e.g. rnorm(n, mean = fit, sd = sigma) gaussian, rpois(n, lambda = fit) Poisson), just conditional mean. needs family-dispatched noise-generation step, likely via internal generic keyed family(model)$family. Naming: functions prefixed lr_ (logistic regression specific). Proposed rename scheme (open discussion): lr_model() → glm_model(), lr_predict() → glm_predict(), lr_simulator() → glm_simulator(), lr_scm_*() → glm_scm_*(), lr_vpc_sim() → glm_vpc_sim(). Class erlr_glm → erglm_model (similar). erplots dispatches purely generic name plus whatever class erlr/erglm chooses register, rename entirely internal package – coordination needed erplots beyond updating registerS3method() calls R/er-methods.R. Example data: lr_data binary (ae1/ae2) responses. Demonstrating gaussian/Poisson families well benefit adding continuous /count response column (also useful erplots’ continuous-response work, see PLAN.md).

previous_headings: Generalise from logistic regression to arbitrary glm() families (→ erglm)
title: erlr development plan
text: questions originally left open; now working recommendation implementation isn’t blocked, still debate new information changes calculus. Rename timing. family generalisation erglm rename two separate steps, one PR: generalise behaviour first (existing erlr name), get reviewed tested, rename final, purely mechanical pass right CRAN submission. Rationale: keeps behavioural review separate large, low-risk find--replace diff, means generalisation decision can revisited without re-rename. (matches existing step ordering , rename last.) Default family. renamed, glm_model() default family = stats::gaussian() – .e. match stats::glm()’s default – rather keeping binomial-logit default. package ’s longer logistic-regression-specific shouldn’t quietly special-case binary responses; users fitting logistic models pass family = binomial() explicitly, base glm(). predictable choice anyone coming base R, cost breaking change existing lr_model() callers – acceptable given rename already breaks call sites. v1 family scope. Explicitly support test four families cover common exposure-response use cases: binomial (binary AE), poisson (count AE), gaussian (continuous biomarker), Gamma (skewed continuous/time-based endpoints). families (inverse.gaussian, quasi-families, etc.) work generic mechanisms “untested, officially supported” someone actually needs one – don’t build speculative test matrix families current use case. SCM test selection. Automatic default, escape hatch: pick test family’s dispersion behaviour ("Chisq" binomial/poisson, known dispersion; "F" gaussian/Gamma/inverse.gaussian/quasi*, estimated dispersion), expose test = c("auto", "Chisq", "F") forward/backward SCM functions users can override . follows convention car::Anova() similar tools use, avoids silently giving wrong-flavoured p-values less common families. VPC noise-model dispatch. Implement , rather punting documented limitation – VPCs core diagnostic domain, “expectation ” VPC materially weaker continuous/count responses (understates predictive uncertainty ignoring residual noise entirely). Scope initial implementation four families (3): Bernoulli/probability draws binomial, rpois() poisson, rnorm() (using estimated residual SD) gaussian, rgamma() (using estimated shape/dispersion) Gamma. Families outside set raise informative error rather silently falling back expectation-shortcut.

previous_headings: Generalise from logistic regression to arbitrary glm() families (→ erglm)
title: erlr development plan
text: Add family argument lr_model(); generalise .as_erlr(). Generalise er_summary.erlr_glm()’s p-value column lookup. Generalise SCM’s test statistic selection. Design implement family-dispatched VPC noise generation. Expand lr_data (add second example dataset) continuous/count responses; expand tests across families. Execute erglm rename (package name, exported function names, class names, DESCRIPTION/NAMESPACE/pkgdown/README/vignettes, GitHub repo).

title: Modelling
text: Creating model:
code: mod <- lr_model(formula = ae1 ~ aucss, data = lr_data) mod #>  #> Call:  stats::glm(formula = formula, family = stats::binomial(link = "logit"),  #>     data = data) #>  #> Coefficients: #> (Intercept)        aucss   #>   -1.791383     0.005497   #>  #> Degrees of Freedom: 299 Total (i.e. Null);  298 Residual #> Null Deviance:       402.1  #> Residual Deviance: 193.4     AIC: 197.4

title: Modelling
text: lr_predict() function produces model predictions: confidence level can adjusted using conf_level argument

title: Modelling
text: two functions control SCM regression, lr_scm_forward() lr_scm_backward(): extract log, use lr_scm_history():
code: base_mod <- lr_model(formula = ae1 ~ aucss, data = lr_data) candidates <- c("sex", "dose", "weight", "age")  final_mod <- base_mod |>    lr_scm_forward(candidates, threshold = 0.01, seed = 3425) |>    lr_scm_backward(candidates, threshold = 0.001, seed = 9821)  final_mod #>  #> Call:  stats::glm(formula = formula, family = stats::binomial(link = "logit"),  #>     data = data) #>  #> Coefficients: #> (Intercept)        aucss   #>   -1.791383     0.005497   #>  #> Degrees of Freedom: 299 Total (i.e. Null);  298 Residual #> Null Deviance:       402.1  #> Residual Deviance: 193.4     AIC: 197.4 lr_scm_history(final_mod) #> # A tibble: 5 × 11 #>   iteration attempt step       action term_tested model_tested   model_converged #>       <int>   <int> <chr>      <chr>  <chr>       <chr>          <lgl>           #> 1         0       0 base model NA     NA          ae1 ~ aucss    TRUE            #> 2         1       1 forward    add    ~sex        ae1 ~ aucss +… TRUE            #> 3         1       2 forward    add    ~weight     ae1 ~ aucss +… TRUE            #> 4         1       3 forward    add    ~age        ae1 ~ aucss +… TRUE            #> 5         1       4 forward    add    ~dose       ae1 ~ aucss +… TRUE            #> # ℹ 4 more variables: term_p_value <dbl>, model_aic <dbl>, model_bic <dbl>, #> #   model_updated <int>

what: Exposure-Response Tools for Logistic Regression Models
title: Exposure-Response Tools for Logistic Regression Models
text: Provides estimation tools exposure-response models use logistic regression binary responses. mostly intended convenience package: core tools wrappers around glm(). plotting exposure-response models (including fitted erlr), see companion package erplots, supplies model-agnostic mini-language building exposure-response plots.

title: Exposure-Response Tools for Logistic Regression Models
text: can install development version erlr like :
code: pak::pak("djnavarro/erlr")

title: Exposure-Response Tools for Logistic Regression Models
code: library(erlr) library(tibble)  lr_data #> # A tibble: 300 × 10 #>       id sex      age weight  dose treatment aucss cmaxss   ae1   ae2 #>    <int> <fct>  <int>  <dbl> <dbl> <fct>     <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #>  1     1 Male      35     79   200 Drug       673.   97.3     0     1 #>  2     2 Female    22     58   200 Drug      2806.  301.      1     1 #>  3     3 Female    28     58     0 Placebo      0     0       0     0 #>  4     4 Female    18     57   100 Drug      1169.  198.      1     1 #>  5     5 Male      28     77   100 Drug       377.   51.4     0     0 #>  6     6 Female    19     76   200 Drug       327.   25.4     1     0 #>  7     7 Male      30     70     0 Placebo      0     0       0     0 #>  8     8 Female    34     60   100 Drug      1208.  133.      1     1 #>  9     9 Male      21     89     0 Placebo      0     0       0     0 #> 10    10 Female    34     56   200 Drug       254.   31.0     0     0 #> # ℹ 290 more rows  mod <- lr_model(ae1 ~ aucss, lr_data) mod #>  #> Call:  stats::glm(formula = formula, family = stats::binomial(link = "logit"),  #>     data = data) #>  #> Coefficients: #> (Intercept)        aucss   #>   -1.791383     0.005497   #>  #> Degrees of Freedom: 299 Total (i.e. Null);  298 Residual #> Null Deviance:       402.1  #> Residual Deviance: 193.4     AIC: 197.4

title: Exposure-Response Tools for Logistic Regression Models
code: mod1 <- lr_model(ae1 ~ aucss + sex + dose, lr_data) mod2 <- lr_scm_backward(mod1, candidates = c("sex", "dose")) #> Using seed = 4292 lr_scm_history(mod2) #> # A tibble: 4 × 11 #>   iteration attempt step       action term_tested model_tested   model_converged #>       <int>   <int> <chr>      <chr>  <chr>       <chr>          <lgl>           #> 1         0       0 base model <NA>   <NA>        ae1 ~ aucss +… TRUE            #> 2         1       1 backward   remove ~sex        ae1 ~ aucss +… TRUE            #> 3         1       2 backward   remove ~dose       ae1 ~ aucss +… TRUE            #> 4         2       3 backward   remove ~sex        ae1 ~ aucss    TRUE            #> # ℹ 4 more variables: term_p_value <dbl>, model_aic <dbl>, model_bic <dbl>, #> #   model_updated <int>

code: mod <- lr_model(ae1 ~ aucss + sex, lr_data) sim <- lr_vpc_sim(mod, seed = 1234) sim #> # A tibble: 30,000 × 5 #>      ae1 aucss sex    row_id sim_id #>    <dbl> <dbl> <fct>   <int>  <int> #>  1 0.894  673. Male        1      1 #>  2 1.000 2806. Female      2      1 #>  3 0.110    0  Female      3      1 #>  4 0.993 1169. Female      4      1 #>  5 0.588  377. Male        5      1 #>  6 0.468  327. Female      6      1 #>  7 0.129    0  Male        7      1 #>  8 0.994 1208. Female      8      1 #>  9 0.129    0  Male        9      1 #> 10 0.362  254. Female     10      1 #> # ℹ 29,990 more rows

title: Stepwise covariate modelling for logistic regression — lr_scm
text: mod erlr model object candidates Character vector list candidate terms threshold Threshold test seed Optional seed control order term tests

title: Stepwise covariate modelling for logistic regression — lr_scm
text: lr_scm_forward() lr_scm_backward(), updated erlr model returned, SCM history log updated internally. lr_scm_history(), data frame returned containing SCM history log

title: Stepwise covariate modelling for logistic regression — lr_scm
code: mod0 <- lr_model(ae1 ~ aucss, lr_data) mod1 <- lr_scm_forward(mod0, candidates = c("sex", "dose")) #> Using seed = 6292 lr_scm_history(mod1) #> # A tibble: 3 × 11 #>   iteration attempt step       action term_tested model_tested   model_converged #>       <int>   <int> <chr>      <chr>  <chr>       <chr>          <lgl>           #> 1         0       0 base model NA     NA          ae1 ~ aucss    TRUE            #> 2         1       1 forward    add    ~sex        ae1 ~ aucss +… TRUE            #> 3         1       2 forward    add    ~dose       ae1 ~ aucss +… TRUE            #> # ℹ 4 more variables: term_p_value <dbl>, model_aic <dbl>, model_bic <dbl>, #> #   model_updated <int>  mod2 <- lr_model(ae1 ~ aucss + sex + dose, lr_data) mod3 <- lr_scm_backward(mod2, candidates = c("sex", "dose")) #> Using seed = 6526 lr_scm_history(mod3) #> # A tibble: 4 × 11 #>   iteration attempt step       action term_tested model_tested   model_converged #>       <int>   <int> <chr>      <chr>  <chr>       <chr>          <lgl>           #> 1         0       0 base model NA     NA          ae1 ~ aucss +… TRUE            #> 2         1       1 backward   remove ~dose       ae1 ~ aucss +… TRUE            #> 3         1       2 backward   remove ~sex        ae1 ~ aucss +… TRUE            #> 4         2       3 backward   remove ~sex        ae1 ~ aucss    TRUE            #> # ℹ 4 more variables: term_p_value <dbl>, model_aic <dbl>, model_bic <dbl>, #> #   model_updated <int>

what: Simulate from a logistic regression model — lr_simulator
title: Simulate from a logistic regression model — lr_simulator
text: Simulate logistic regression model

title: Simulate from a logistic regression model — lr_simulator
code: lr_simulator(object)

title: Simulate from a logistic regression model — lr_simulator
text: object logistic regression model

title: Simulate from a logistic regression model — lr_simulator
text: function arguments param, data, type. param argument vector coefficients data argument data frame tibble type argument string indicating type prediction generate (defaults "response") Takes fitted glm object input returns function evaluate underlying structural model user-specified parameters data (e.g., VPCs counterfactual simulation scenarios). principle work glms generally, merely logistic regressions, tested except logistic regression models

title: Simulate from a logistic regression model — lr_simulator
code: mod1 <- lr_model(ae2 ~ aucss + sex, lr_data) par1 <- coef(mod1) mod1_sim <- lr_simulator(mod1)  # no counterfactuals p1 <- mod1_sim(param = par1, data = lr_data)  p2 <- unname(predict(mod1, type = "response")) # same result  # user modifies the data set lr_data2 <- lr_data[1:20, ] p3 <- mod1_sim(param = par1, data = lr_data2)  p4 <- unname(predict(mod1, newdata = lr_data2, type = "response")) # same result  # user modifies the parameters par2 <- par1 int1 <- par1["(Intercept)"] par2["(Intercept)"] <- 0 p5 <- mod1_sim(param = par2, data = lr_data)

Male        1      1 #>  2 0.990  2806. Female      2      1 #>  3 0.124     0  Female      3      1 #>  4 0.680  1169. Female      4      1 #>  5 0.173   377. Male        5      1 #>  6 0.232   327. Female      6      1 #>  7 0.0801    0  Male        7      1 #>  8 0.700  1208. Female      8      1 #>  9 0.0801    0  Male        9      1 #> 10 0.203   254. Female     10      1 #> # ℹ 29,990 more rows
